document.heading: Authorization to Negotiate and Enter into Loan Agreement
document.preamble: At a duly called Special Session of the Upper Mattaponi Tribal Council held this 12th day of March, 2026, the following Resolution and Law were adopted.

recital.text: the Upper Mattaponi Tribe (the "Tribe") is an Indian Tribe that has existed from time immemorial and is federally recognized on a government-to-government basis by the United States of America; and,

the Constitution of the Upper Mattaponi Tribe (the "Constitution") was adopted to ensure the Tribe's well-being by fostering self-sufficiency, promoting sustainable development, and securing the economic future of the Tribe and its citizens; and,

Article VII, Section 4(a) of the Constitution vests legislative power in the Council of the Upper Mattaponi Tribe (the "Council") to enact laws and budget resolutions that support these interests; and,
the Tribe is planning for certain construction projects and has appropriated funds for that purpose, Budget Resolution No. 2026-01; and
the Tribe anticipates the need to borrow funds in the form of a loan for the budgeted construction project.

that the Council hereby authorizes the Chief of the Tribe, or his designee, to negotiate and enter into a business loan for the identified construction activities in an amount not to exceed five million dollars ($5,000,000.00).

procedural-data.text: The foregoing Resolution was duly voted upon by the Council on the 12th day of March, 2026, at a Special Session with a vote of 7 in favor and 0 opposed, 0 abstaining, and 0 absent, pursuant to the authority vested in the Council by the Constitution of the Upper Mattaponi Tribe.
